facts.text: Hudson was the first city in the United States to be incorporated after the thirteen colonies became a nation.
Historic Hudson, a nonprofit founded in 1996, protects the town’s unique architectural heritage and keeps its historic buildings in prime condition.
The 1850 railroad across the Hudson River cut off South Bay and North Bay harbors, reshaping the town’s maritime trade.
Hudson is called a “dictionary of architectural history” because its streets showcase a wealth of restored, exemplary buildings.
Jazz enthusiasts can catch the annual JazzFest at Hudson Hall, while art lovers visit Olana, the historic home of Hudson River School painter Thomas Cole.
During the 1800s, Diamond Street hosted secret gambling dens and brothels, earning Hudson a reputation for underground entertainment.
